facts.value: HTTPie CLI: human-friendly HTTP client for the API era
HTTPie is a command-line HTTP client designed for testing, debugging, and generally interacting with APIs and HTTP servers.
The `http` and `https` commands allow creating and sending arbitrary HTTP requests.
HTTPie uses simple and natural syntax and provides formatted and colorized output.

Features include expressive syntax, formatted/colorized output, built-in JSON support, forms and file uploads, HTTPS, proxies, authentication, arbitrary request data, custom headers, persistent sessions, and wget-like downloads.
README examples include `https httpie.io/hello`, `http PUT pie.dev/put X-API-Token:123 name=John`, `http --offline pie.dev/post hello=offline`, and a GitHub API authenticated POST example.
README links to installation instructions at https://httpie.io/docs#installation and full documentation at https://httpie.io/docs.
setup.cfg declares console scripts for `http`, `https`, and `httpie`.
README links to the HTTPie website, Discord, Twitter, StackOverflow tag, GitHub Issues, newsletter, and contribution guide.

product.name: HTTPie CLI
product.oneLiner: A human-friendly command-line HTTP client for the API era.
audiences: Developers testing and debugging HTTP services
problems: Developers need to create and inspect arbitrary HTTP requests from the terminal.
API debugging benefits from syntax and output optimized for humans rather than raw transport details.
features: Simple and natural syntax for HTTP requests
Forms, file uploads, custom headers, authentication, proxies, and persistent sessions
Offline mode for building and printing requests without sending them
useCases: PUT JSON data with a custom API token.
Preview a request in offline mode.
Post to the GitHub API with authentication.
examples: http -a USERNAME POST https://api.github.com/repos/httpie/cli/issues/83/comments body='HTTPie is awesome! :heart:'

notes: README links to CONTRIBUTING.md and GitHub Issues for feature requests and bug reports.

gaps.message: README links to installation docs rather than embedding one canonical install command.
The README includes a prominent star-loss note that a generated public site may want to handle separately from product onboarding.
